title: Implement generic providers
body:
The intial refactor used specifically qemu for testing and infra bring up.  But the whole point was to have things interfaced.  This PR results in an interface experience like podman 4 using the same term `provider` to generically represent 'a provider' like qemu/applehv/etc.

This PR is required to move forward with new providers.

Also renamed pkg/machine/p5 to pkg/machine/shim.

[NO NEW TESTS REQUIRED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/machine-dev-5/OWNERS)~~ [baude,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> One comment, otherwise LGTM.
> 
> I think you need top remove [CI:MACHINE] before we can merge though?

it will merge with a /lgtm ... i believe it did last time.  if folks really want this, i can repush
fwiw, i removed it on gh but i dont think it matters unless i re-push which is my hesitation.
It will merge but you're not actually running most of the tests so we risk regressions.

Yes, please (and thanks) re-push w/o `[CI:MACHINE]`.  I think the branch-protection for this branch isn't enabled?  I know it's a pain, but hopefully much less of a pain than if something were to slip into `main` or `machine-dev-5`.  Tracking that down would likely waste much more time than waiting for another CI run.

Update: As suspected, there was no branch-protection enabled for `machine-dev-5`.  I fixed that (used same setup as for `main`).  Hopefully that means slash-lgtm will no-longer merge w/o `Total Success` being green.  Whew :cold_sweat:

this is a dev branch, there are no expectations like main ... but thanks for the effort.   all of this code will have to cherry picked back into main which is where I expect all the quality checks to occur.

> this is a dev branch

Ooooohhhh! I don't know why but that completely flew past me, it's even in the name (durrrr)  You're right, thanks for point it out.  It will all get checked again when it goes into main.  Sorry for the false alarm bells.

Feel free to disable branch protection if it's getting in the way.  Though...it could still be beneficial in perhaps reducing some load during cherry-picking?  In other words, it may be helpful to know incrementally that CI is clean.  Up to you.
